meaning: n.<美俚> 笨手笨脚的人,木头人,不善交际的人,傻瓜
examples: If we had every klutz jump into the orchestra pit, or prance on the opera stage, or slop paint with Picasso, we would have some great parties but no art.
Why are you so clumsy, you klutz?
Im such as klutz. I trip on my own feet all the time!
Words of Yiddish origin include bagel, lox, blintze, nosh, kosher, kibitz, klutz, tush, schlemiel, spiel and schlep.
There's no reason to doubt Robertson's story, and the good-guy pitcher swears up and down that, in fact, he is simply a klutz.
